main.description: An arbitrary, game-specific configuration value chosen by the player, for use in a play record's `state` array. Use `games.gamesgamesgamesgames.state.setup` for the universal pre-run choices (mode, seed, character, difficulty); use this for everything else. Distinct from `state.metric`, which tracks values that change as the run progresses. Exactly one of `value`, `intValue`, `boolValue` or `dataValue` should be set. Cardinality: keyed — unique per ($type, id); writers upsert by `id`.

id.description: Stable, game-defined camelCase identifier for this setting. Language-independent and consistent across play-throughs & game versions.

name.description: Human-readable display name in the locale active at write time. Optional, but recommended so renderers without a localised mapping of `id` can fall back to displaying this.

value.description: The setting's value, when it is a string.

intValue.description: The setting's value, when it is an integer.

boolValue.description: The setting's value, when it is a boolean.

dataValue.description: The setting's value, when it is structured data. Must be an object at the top level, per the atproto data model.
